data.title: Yi He: Binance Moves Beyond Crypto and TradFi Labels, Targets Global Financial Infrastructure
data.description: Binance co-founder Yi He said the exchange has launched commodity trading — including crude oil, gold, and silver — with volumes already accounting for a significant share of traditional market scale, as the company moves to position itself as global financial infrastructure beyond crypto or TradFi categories. Speaking at the Hong Kong Web3 Festival 2026, Yi said the industry has evolved from its early "take down Wall Street" posture to a convergence phase, citing Futu, Tiger Brokers, and Robinhood as platforms now holding both equity and crypto assets. Yi said Binance's long-term direction is guided by first principles: advancing global financial freedom, financial equality, and improving the lives of ordinary users.

data.title: Asian stock markets rose as investors bet that the "peak of uncertainty" had passed.
data.description: Asian stocks rose on Monday as investors put aside renewed tensions in the Middle East over the weekend and refocused on fundamentals and the prospect of further negotiations. The MSCI Asia Pacific index rose as much as 0.8%, while the MSCI Emerging Markets index recovered all losses suffered in nearly two months since the start of the conflict in Iran. Analysts and strategists said these developments suggest investors are now betting that the peak of uncertainty has passed. They cited factors such as a more optimistic outlook for negotiations, a recovery in AI-related trading, and a renewed focus on corporate fundamentals as earnings season begins as drivers of improved market sentiment. (Jinshi)

data.title: Vitalik envisions Ethereum's future: evolving from a transaction chain into a technological foundation, thereby achieving true user sovereignty.
data.description: At the 2026 Hong Kong Web3 Carnival, Vitalik Buterin stated that Ethereum's ultimate goal is to build a digital infrastructure with "self-sovereignty" where users do not need to trust any intermediary, supporting various decentralized applications from finance and social networking to identity verification, and serving as a trust anchor for off-chain applications (such as privacy voting, prediction markets, and decentralized social networking).

data.title: New Zealand PM Calls for Bipartisan Support on India Trade Deal
data.description: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon has called for bipartisan backing to secure a free trade agreement with India. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Luxon's appeal for cross-party cooperation to strengthen economic ties with India. The Prime Minister emphasized the potential benefits of such an agreement, which could enhance trade and investment opportunities between the two nations. Luxon stressed the importance of a united political front to advance negotiations and achieve a favorable outcome for New Zealand's economy. The call for bipartisan support comes amid ongoing efforts to diversify New Zealand's trade partnerships and reduce reliance on traditional markets.

data.title: STOCKS | Coal Prices Near Key Threshold Amid Market Concerns
data.description:
On April 20, Jin10 reported that Du Cong, the chief coal analyst at Zhongtai Securities, released a research report addressing market concerns over the potential collapse of coal prices due to the reopening of the Taiwan Strait. According to Jin10, data indicates that mid-April saw the market price of thermal coal reach 769 yuan per ton, approaching the critical 800 yuan mark, with a weekly increase of 8 yuan. Coking coal prices rose by 70 yuan per ton weekly, driven by strong non-electricity demand.

The demand side shows a dual resonance between electricity and non-electricity sectors: off-season power coal consumption increased by 7% year-on-year, daily steel iron production averaged 2.45 million tons, and weekly coal consumption in coal chemical industries reached 7.5 million tons, up from 7 million tons last year. Additionally, reduced output from hydropower and renewable sources has bolstered thermal power support.

In terms of inventory, power plant stocks decreased by 0.6% year-on-year, reaching a three-year low, while port and coking coal inventories plummeted by 15.8% year-on-year, indicating a supply-demand shift favoring sellers. Despite this, stock prices have not risen in tandem with coal prices, as market expectations remain anchored at 800 yuan per ton. Should there be a significant weekly increase of 30 yuan or a breakthrough beyond 800 yuan, stock prices may rebound quickly, presenting a golden window for investment.

data.title: Germany's March PPI Shows Smaller Decline Than Expected
data.description: Germany's Producer Price Index (PPI) for March recorded a year-on-year decrease of 0.2%, according to Jin10. This figure was better than the anticipated decline of 0.5% and showed an improvement from the previous month's drop of 3.3%. The data suggests a potential stabilization in producer prices, which could have implications for inflation trends and economic forecasts in the region.

data.title: Germany's March PPI Surpasses Expectations with 2.5% Increase
data.description: Germany's Producer Price Index (PPI) for March showed a significant increase, recording a monthly rate of 2.5%. According to Jin10, this figure exceeded the anticipated rate of 1.3% and marked a notable rise from the previous month's rate of -0.50%. The data suggests a stronger-than-expected inflationary pressure within the German economy during this period.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Global Silver Deficit Projected to Widen by 15% in 2026
data.description: The global silver market is facing a potential widening of its deficit, according to Kobeissi Letter. The deficit is expected to increase by 15% in 2026, reaching 46 million troy ounces, marking the sixth consecutive year of shortfall. According to NS3.AI, global silver stocks have been depleted by 762 million troy ounces since 2021. Additionally, demand for silver coins and bars is anticipated to rise by 18% year over year, while total supply may decrease by 2%.

data.title: Wang Chuan from Silicon Valley sold 233,300 HYPE tokens, making a profit of over $1.18 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Chuan (@Svwang1) of Silicon Valley sold 233,296 HYPE tokens at a price of $41.46, receiving $9.67 million in USDC, making a profit of over $1.18 million. Chuan still holds 8,529 HYPE tokens, worth $348,700.

data.title: PEPE earned $11.9 million, and a whale-related address spent $245,000 to purchase ASTEROID.
data.description: Gmgn data shows that an address spent $245,000 to purchase ASTEROID, holding 0.18% of the token. Furthermore, on-chain data suggests that this address may be associated with the PEPE whale dimethyltryptamine.eth. This address had multiple financial transactions with the whale 2-3 years ago, and dimethyltryptamine.eth has a total profit of $11.9 million on PEPE, currently holding $7.2 million.

data.title: Yi He at HK Web3 Festival: All Global Finance Will Eventually Run on Blockchain
data.description: Binance co-founder Yi He, speaking at the Hong Kong Web3 Festival 2026, outlined three strategic priorities: the intersection of AI with blockchain and finance, which she described as an unmissable moment in human history; organizational efficiency and talent development as Binance scales beyond founder-driven growth; and the deepening convergence of crypto with traditional finance. Yi said future global foreign exchange transactions will migrate to blockchain, displacing the SWIFT system, and that asset trading will eventually operate on a 24/7 global basis mirroring today's crypto markets, as the broader financial industry enters a period of sweeping, accelerating transformation.

data.description: 1. Trump retweeted an article: Trump has won the Iraq War, achieving nine major breakthroughs; 2. Vitalik Buterin: Ethereum's four-year roadmap focuses on scaling and quantum security; 3. LayerZero: The KelpDAO attack was limited to its rsETH configuration and the protocol itself has no vulnerabilities; 4. Bitcoin spot ETFs saw net inflows of $996 million last week, marking the third consecutive week of net inflows; 5. A whale withdrew 98,000 wstETH and 3,000 cbBTC from Aave; 6. Polymarket plans to raise $400 million at a $15 billion valuation; 7. The BLESS project team has sold a total of 400 million BLESS tokens, worth approximately $4.43 million.

data.title: Starknet Mainnet Temporarily Unavailable for Upgrade
data.description: Starknet announced that its mainnet will be temporarily unavailable starting at 16:10 UTC+8 today for the v0.14.2 upgrade. According to Foresight News, the downtime is expected to last approximately 10 minutes. This upgrade introduces proof verification within the protocol, enabling native proof verification without relying on application layer solutions. This development unlocks native privacy capabilities and supports STRK20 and strkBTC, while also laying the groundwork for zkThreads and deeper decentralization.

data.title: HYPE Price Declines as BTC/HYPE Exchange Rate Rises
data.description:
On April 20, HYPE's price experienced a 6.3% decline, according to Hyperinsight monitoring. According to BlockBeats, the BTC/HYPE exchange rate increased by 7% over the past 24 hours, currently standing at 1821. A significant whale on Hyperliquid, heavily invested in this exchange rate, earned $900,000 in daily profits by betting on BTC's resilience compared to altcoins.

The whale's current holdings include a BTC long position with 40x leverage, valued at $31.4 million at an average price of $70,557, and a HYPE short position with 5x leverage, valued at $22.58 million at an average price of $38.93.

This address began establishing its positions during HYPE's previous surge, completing its setup by March 20, with a total scale exceeding $53 million. The strategy appears to be a bet on a broader market downturn, with HYPE expected to decline more significantly than BTC. The current net profit from these combined positions is $500,000.

Address: 0x939f95036d2e7b6d7419ec072bf9d967352204d2

data.title: Ukraine Open to Potential Meeting Between Zelensky and Putin in Turkey
data.description:
Ukrainian Foreign Minister Andriy Sybiha announced last Sunday that President Volodymyr Zelensky is prepared for a potential meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Turkey. According to Odaily, Sybiha made this statement during an interview with the Ukrainian National News Agency on the sidelines of the fifth Antalya Diplomacy Forum.

Sybiha highlighted Turkey's capacity to achieve unprecedented diplomatic success, stating, "We have urged Turkey to consider organizing a meeting at the level of Zelensky and Putin, with the possibility of Turkish President Erdogan and U.S. President Donald Trump also participating."

He emphasized that the Ukrainian side, including Zelensky, is ready for such a meeting and mentioned that Kyiv has put forward "practical proposals." Sybiha expressed hope that Turkey could play a role in accelerating the peace process surrounding the Russia-Ukraine conflict.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin: Ethereum's four-year roadmap focuses on scaling and quantum security.
data.description: At the Web3 Carnival in Hong Kong, Vitalik Buterin stated that the two core functions of the Ethereum protocol are as a public bulletin board and as a shared digital object. The value of decentralized applications lies in ensuring autonomy, security, verifiability, fair participation, and user aggregation. The four-year roadmap includes short-term scaling, the launch of ZKVM to support more complex computations, early preparations for post-quantum cryptography, and account abstraction to support smart contract wallets and quantum-resistant signatures. The long-term goal is to maximize secure consensus, formal verification, extreme simplicity, and quantum security. Final determinism will be achieved within 1 to 3 slots, and ZKVM will support device verification for mobile phones and other devices, with the goal of achieving large-scale scaling by 2028. Ethereum's goal is to become the most secure and decentralized chain.

data.title: SBI Ripple Asia Launches Prepaid Payment Token Platform on XRP Ledger
data.description: SBI Ripple Asia has introduced a prepaid payment token platform on the XRP Ledger, aiming to capture a share of Japan's approximately 30 trillion yen (around $200 billion) prepaid payment market. According to NS3.AI, Tobu Top Tours will be the inaugural issuer, offering Japanese-yen-backed tourism payment tokens for use in hotels, transportation, and attractions. Fintech Observer reported that SBI Ripple Asia became a registered third-party prepaid payment instrument issuer under Japan's Payment Services Act on March 26.

data.title: Circle and Tether Expand Trademark Coverage in South Korea
data.description: Circle and Tether, both prominent stablecoin issuers, have expanded their trademark coverage in South Korea. According to NS3.AI, Circle filed a total of 11 trademarks, while Tether filed six trademarks in the region. Data from KIPRIS dated April 20 reveals that Circle submitted three trademarks this year and eight last year. Meanwhile, Tether filed two trademarks this year and two last year, indicating a strategic move to strengthen their presence in the South Korean market.

data.title: LayerZero Reports Attack Due to Ignored Security Recommendations
data.description:
LayerZero has disclosed that attackers managed to compromise two RPC nodes relied upon by the company's verifier, leading to a successful attack. According to CoinDesk, the attackers also launched a Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attack on the remaining nodes. The breach was facilitated by Kelp's decision to overlook multi-verifier recommendations, which are designed to enhance security measures and prevent such vulnerabilities.

LayerZero's incident highlights the importance of adhering to security protocols and recommendations, especially in the context of blockchain technology where the integrity of nodes is crucial. The attack underscores the risks associated with relying on a limited number of verifiers, as it can create single points of failure that are susceptible to exploitation. By ignoring the multi-verifier recommendations, Kelp inadvertently exposed the system to vulnerabilities that the attackers were able to exploit effectively.

The situation serves as a reminder for companies in the blockchain space to prioritize robust security frameworks and ensure that all recommended protocols are followed diligently. As the industry continues to evolve, the need for comprehensive security measures becomes increasingly critical to safeguard against potential threats and maintain the trust of users and stakeholders.

data.title: Lending and Yield Protocols Experience Significant TVL Declines
data.description:
Several lending and yield protocols are experiencing notable double-digit percentage decreases in their total value locked (TVL). According to CoinDesk, despite these substantial declines in TVL, the prices of tokens associated with these protocols have only seen limited reductions. This trend highlights a divergence between the value locked in these platforms and the market valuation of their tokens.

The decline in TVL across these protocols suggests a shift in user behavior or confidence, potentially driven by broader market conditions or specific issues within the protocols themselves. However, the relatively stable token prices indicate that investors may still hold a degree of confidence in the long-term viability of these platforms. This situation underscores the complex dynamics at play in the cryptocurrency market, where various factors can influence different aspects of a protocol's ecosystem. As the market continues to evolve, stakeholders will be closely monitoring these trends to assess their potential impact on the broader crypto landscape.
